source.title: Machine learning-guided co-optimization of fitness and diversity facilitates combinatorial library design in enzyme engineering

initial_request.target_name: Generalist Rma cytochrome c biocatalysts for C-B and C-Si bond formation
initial_request.input_data: Crystal structure of Rma cytochrome c (PDB 6CUK), six targeted active-site/front-loop residues (75, 99, 100, 101, 102, 103), prior directed-evolution knowledge for borylation and silylation, and MODIFY Pareto library design algorithm.
initial_request.user_intent: Engineer cytochrome c variants that can catalyze both carbon-boron and carbon-silicon bond formation with strong activity and stereocontrol, while exploring a broader and more diverse sequence space than standard saturation libraries.
initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Produce a combinatorial library whose screened variants outperform an NNK control in both reactions, including at least about 2x higher average yield and improved enantiomeric ratio, while uncovering novel high-performing generalist variants.

evidence.support: The paper states the aim of engineering cytochrome c variants that catalyze both C-B and C-Si bond formation with excellent efficiency and stereocontrol.

agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Known / completed: Existing Rma cytochrome c variants can catalyze new-to-nature carbene-transfer reactions, but the best evolved enzymes are reaction-specific. [Gap] Missing / unresolved: A compact library that targets the most relevant positions while preserving enough diversity to discover generalist catalysts. [Decision] Use the Rma cyt c crystal structure to define six targeted residues around the active site and front loop for combinatorial optimization, expecting these hotspots to control both activity and stereoselectivity.
agent_trajectory.observation: The study fixed on six active-site/front-loop residues as the designable positions for library construction.

evidence.support: The figure caption identifies residues 75, 99, 100, 101, 102, and 103 as the six targeted residues used to create the combinatorial libraries.

agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Known / completed: Six hotspots were selected for diversification. [Gap] Missing / unresolved: Standard degenerate libraries either waste budget on nonfunctional variants or collapse onto a narrow region of sequence space. [Decision] Apply the MODIFY Pareto optimization algorithm, with optional residue-level priors, to co-optimize predicted fitness and sequence diversity when constructing the starting library.
agent_trajectory.observation: MODIFY generated a Pareto-style library design intended to populate multiple high-fitness regions rather than a single narrow optimum.

evidence.support: The paper describes MODIFY as balancing fitness and diversity and allowing residue-level diversity control with prior knowledge.
The methods section states that MODIFY designs combinatorial libraries with optimized sequence fitness and diversity over selected residues.

agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Known / completed: A MODIFY-informed library has been designed. [Gap] Missing / unresolved: Whether this library actually yields better catalysts than a standard NNK control in both reaction classes. [Decision] Randomly sample and screen 160 clones from the MODIFY library in both C-B and C-Si bond-forming reactions, quantifying yield and enantiomeric ratio by chiral HPLC.
agent_trajectory.observation: Across both assays, the MODIFY library outperformed the NNK control, indicating that the designed library was enriched for productive catalysts.

evidence.support: The paper reports random selection of 160 clones, dual screening in C-B and C-Si reactions, and chiral HPLC measurement of yield and enantiomeric ratio.

agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Known / completed: MODIFY screening enriched for productive variants. [Gap] Missing / unresolved: Which variants best combine novelty, catalytic activity, and stereocontrol across both transformations. [Decision] Rank the screened variants by reaction performance and compare them to previously evolved RDT and TDE baselines, expecting novel hits rather than simple rediscovery of known solutions.
agent_trajectory.observation: The library yielded strong C-B and C-Si catalysts, including novel variants six mutations away from the previously evolved RDT and TDE enzymes; MLYPPT emerged as a particularly important generalist hit.

evidence.support: The paper states that the best-performing borylation and silylation variants are six mutations away from previously evolved RDT and TDE variants.
The paper states that MODIFY produced the generalist MLYPPT variant with C-B and C-Si performance superior or comparable to experimentally evolved enzymes.

agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Known / completed: Novel high-performing variants have been identified, including double-proline front-loop mutants. [Gap] Missing / unresolved: Why these variants can support two different carbene-transfer reactions. [Decision] Run molecular dynamics analysis on top MODIFY variants to test whether front-loop conformational changes and flexibility explain reaction generality.
agent_trajectory.observation: MD showed substantial front-loop conformational and dynamic changes, supporting the idea that increased loop flexibility helps generalist variants accommodate both substrates.

evidence.support: The paper reports significant front-loop conformational changes in MODIFY variants and links improved loop flexibility of MLYPPT to generality across both reactions.
The paper identifies functional double-proline mutants including MLYPPT and SPIPAM with excellent catalytic activity and enantioselectivity.

success_verification.validation_technique: Whole-cell and cell-free biocatalysis assays for C-B and C-Si bond formation, followed by chiral HPLC quantification of yield and enantiomeric ratio, plus MD-based structural interpretation of top hits.

C-B average yield improvement.interpretation: The MODIFY library delivered a 2.2-fold higher average yield than the NNK control in carbon-boron bond formation.

C-B average enantiomeric ratio improveme.interpretation: The MODIFY library improved average enantiomeric ratio fourfold in carbon-boron bond formation.

C-Si average yield improvement.interpretation: The MODIFY library delivered a 1.9-fold higher average yield than the NNK control in carbon-silicon bond formation.

C-Si average enantiomeric ratio improvem.interpretation: The MODIFY library improved average enantiomeric ratio 1.3-fold in carbon-silicon bond formation.

success_verification.final_verdict: The study successfully used MODIFY to design a structurally informed, diversity-aware combinatorial library that produced novel and generalist Rma cytochrome c variants for both C-B and C-Si bond formation, outperforming a standard NNK library on key yield and stereocontrol metrics.

evidence.support: The paper reports 2.2-fold and 1.9-fold average yield gains and improved enantiomeric ratios relative to NNK for C-B and C-Si reactions.
The paper concludes that MODIFY enabled a generalist MLYPPT variant with performance superior or comparable to experimentally evolved biocatalysts.

quality.notes: Curated from the CC-BY Nature Communications PDF using page-level evidence from the official open-access article. Curated source file: data/curated/cases/SELT-PROT-0002.json
